channel.title: Finance
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from Finance, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Hulu’s Best Overlooked Show Has 100% Critic, 98% Audience Rotten Tomatoes Scores
item.description: Hulu and Disney Plus have a returning show with near-perfect Rotten Tomatoes scores that seems to be vastly overlooked.

item.title: Should AI Bots Run Their Own Social Media Network? Not If It’s Like Moltbook
item.description: Moltbook is proof that it takes humans to make social media worthwhile.

item.title: Google Gemini boss describes working with founders Larry Page and Sergey Brin to win the AI future
item.description: Demis Hassabis told Fortune it's a return to the "shipping culture" from "the golden era of Google."

item.title: Gmail Security Warning — AI Attackers Can Read Your Email
item.description: Gmail users, pay attention: newly reported AI attacks can access and read your email messages.

item.title: The 70/30 rule that separates millionaires from everyone else
item.description: "Sexy is how you go broke," David Bach argues, saying millionaires know something others don't: Boring is the real sexy.

item.title: Quordle Hints And Answers For Friday, February 13
item.description: Looking for some help with today’s Quordle words? Some hints and the answers are right here to give you a hand.

item.title: Microsoft Confirms Windows Is Under Attack — Update Now
item.description: Attackers have not waited for Exploit Wednesday; the Microsoft Windows zero-day attacks have already started. Here’s what you need to know and do.

item.title: ‘Fertility president’ Trump has demanded a baby boom, and Stanford researchers have a solution: letting more people work from home, study finds
item.description: “It seems like just such an obvious thing to do,” remote work expert Nick Bloom said. “As an economist, I don't think I've ever seen the more obvious,...

item.title: Why 50% stay broke and how one hour a day can change everything
item.description: Next time you go to work, David Bach says, try paying yourself first.

item.title: HPE turns finance into the front line of enterprise AI
item.description: HPE’s AI named Alfred is shrinking reporting cycles and rewriting the CFO playbook.

item.title: Gun accessory maker to pay $1.75M to Buffalo mass shooting victims’ families
item.description: A Georgia gun accessory maker will pay $1.75 million to victims' families of the 2022 Buffalo mass shooting and halt New York sales of its MA Lock dev...

item.title: Weekly unemployment-claims data show it’s still a ‘low fire’ jobs market
It appears China been investing in a ‘hack’ of Trump’s tariff regime
Why Harley-Davidson’s debt may now be on the verge of junk status
Quarterly results show the Crocs brand is growing again — overseas, anyway
Dow targets all-time high even as Nasdaq slumps with tech-sector strength sapped
Expert credits Trump tax certainty for economic confidence, Americans returning to workforce
item.description: The American workforce rebounds with unexpected job gains in January, signaling economic momentum as labor participation rises across demographics nat...

item.title: Jaguar Land Rover recalling 2,300 electric vehicles in US over fire risk
item.description: Jaguar Land Rover recalled nearly 2,300 I-Pace electric SUVs over a fire risk from overheating batteries, with owners urged to park outside and limit ...

item.title: FAA lifts El Paso flight restriction after US disables Mexican cartel drones that 'breached US airspace'
item.description: The Federal Aviation Administration walked back an order grounding all flights to and from the El Paso International Airport on Wednesday.

item.title: US economy added 130K jobs in January, delayed report shows
item.description: The U.S. economy added jobs at a steady pace to start the year, as the Labor Department reported that employers hired 130,000 workers in January 2026,...

item.title: Reid Hoffman Raises $24.6 Million for AI Cancer-Research Startup
item.description: The LinkedIn co-founder is starting Manas AI with “The Emperor of All Maladies” author Siddhartha Mukherjee.

item.title: What to Know About China's DeepSeek AI
item.description: The Chinese upstart says it has trained high-performing AI models cheaply, without using the most advanced chips.

item.title: Silicon Valley Is Raving About a Made-in-China AI Model
item.description: DeepSeek is called “amazing and impressive” despite working with less-advanced chips.

item.title: Tech, Media & Telecom Roundup: Market Talk
item.description: DeepSeek, SoFi and more in the latest Market Talks covering Technology, Media and Telecom.

item.title: Can an App Replace a Personal Trainer? I Tested Nearly a Dozen to Find Out
item.description: Apps can add flexibility and freshness to your routine. Just try not to get injured.
